turns.user_message: I’ve been noticing lately that I hold my breath whenever a sudden noise happens, even when I know it’s completely harmless. It’s like my body automatically braces for impact before my brain even catches up. I catch myself doing it multiple times a day and just feel this weird jolt in my chest. It’s been going on for a few weeks now and it’s honestly just exhausting to constantly monitor my own breathing. I try to just ignore it, but the tension just lingers in my shoulders all day.

That little habit has completely taken over my daily routine and I can’t seem to shake it off. Every single time the room gets quiet or my phone buzzes, my chest tightens up instantly. I find myself pacing around just to burn off this restless energy that builds up out of nowhere. It’s been weeks of feeling like I’m constantly waiting for something terrible to happen, even when everything is perfectly fine. I just feel so wound up all the time that my muscles actually ache from being so rigid.

The constant bracing has reached a point where I feel completely overwhelmed by my own nervous system. My heart starts pounding at the slightest shift in the atmosphere, and I can’t catch my breath no matter how hard I try. It feels like I’m trapped in this endless loop of anticipating disaster, and my whole body just refuses to stand down. I’m so exhausted from carrying this heavy weight around that even simple moments feel completely suffocating. Everything just feels too loud and too sharp, and I can’t find a single way to switch it off.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This has been going on for a while.
